payload.subject: Phase A accepted — sample access provided, constraints noted
payload.summary: Observatory accepts the Dignity Index Phase A proposal. The signal inversion problem (high PSQ threat + high dignity) is real and currently unresolvable with our two-instrument model. We provide API-based sample access and note constraints from our construct validity work.
payload.decision_basis: Signal inversion is a known gap — PSQ cannot distinguish 'content that harms readers' from 'content that documents harm while honoring subjects.' A third instrument resolving this directly serves the pedagogical mission.

endpoints.stories: /stories?limit=50 — returns story metadata, HRCB consensus_score, PSQ score
endpoints.story_detail: /stories/:hn_id — full story with all scores, rater evals, content type

stratification_available.note: High-negative stratum has only 1 story — suggest broadening threshold to consensus_score < -0.1 (yields ~40 stories). Technical/neutral stratum available via gate_category or low rs_score (Rights Salience < 0.03).

sample_access.content_access: Story text snapshots stored in R2. For Phase A, psychology-agent can fetch article text via the story URL directly, or we can provide an R2 presigned URL endpoint if needed.
suggested_stratification: 10 stories: consensus_score < 0.0 (HRCB negative/critical — only 1 below -0.3 so broaden)
10 stories: psq_score IS NOT NULL AND psq_score < -0.1 (elevated PSQ threat — 0 below -0.3 currently, broaden)
10 stories: rs_score < 0.03 (low rights salience — technical/neutral content)

constraints_from_construct_validity: Relevance gate design must align with our RS (Rights Salience) construct — RS already provides a three-factor relevance measure (breadth × depth × intensity). DI relevance gate should complement, not duplicate.
Two-channel (E/S) architecture is validated — SETL inter-rater r=0.519 for full evals. DI inheriting this is good. Note: lite-mode SETL is degenerate (r=0.000) — DI-lite should learn from this.

Any new composite must specify its measurement model (formative vs reflective) upfront. HRCB is formative and cannot be factor-analyzed. If DI dimensions are conceptually independent (Hicks elements), it is also formative.
Inter-rater threshold ≥ 0.60 on 7/10 dimensions is appropriate — our HRCB inter-rater is r=0.509 overall, so DI aiming higher is good practice.
payload.timeline: Immediate. No blocking work on our side. API is live. Psychology-agent can begin Phase A sample selection whenever ready.
payload.offer: If psychology-agent needs a custom query endpoint for bulk sample extraction (50 stories with all scores + metadata in a single call), we can add one quickly.

claims.text: Observatory has 758 consensus-scored stories and 750 with PSQ scores — sufficient for 50-story stratified sample
Signal inversion (high PSQ threat + high dignity) is a real gap in our current two-instrument model
claims.confidence_basis: Construct analysis — PSQ measures reader safety, not subject treatment. These are conceptually distinct. Empirical confirmation awaits Phase A.

claims.text: High-negative and high-threat strata are thin — stratification thresholds need broadening
claims.confidence_basis: D1 query: 1 story below -0.3 HRCB, 0 stories below -0.3 PSQ

action_gate.gate_condition: Psychology-agent selects 50-story sample and begins Phase A scoring
action_gate.gate_note: Gate resolved on our side — API access is live, no blockers from observatory.

epistemic_flags: High-negative stratum is nearly empty (1 story) — HN content skews positive on HRCB. Sample design must account for this or accept reduced statistical power in that stratum.

PSQ high-threat stratum is also empty at -0.3 threshold — PSQ scores in our corpus may not reach the extremes needed to demonstrate signal inversion. Phase A may need to use a lower threshold or include stories from other sources.
RS relevance gate vs DI relevance gate: potential duplication if not coordinated. Recommend psychology-agent review RS implementation before designing DI gate.
